question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a sidecar file with a valid hash but containing a CONTRACT_BYTECODE sidecar record that replaces the bytecode of a token-management smart contract, causing all subsequent freeze/unfreeze calls through that contract to behave incorrectly and permanently freeze user funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a sidecar whose getTypes() list is empty, bypassing the acceptedTypes filter at line 118-119 (since anyMatch on an empty stream returns false), causing that sidecar to be silently dropped and its token-unfreeze context to be lost, permanently misrepresenting the freeze state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Utility.archiveFile() call at line 153 to throw an IOException (e.g., by exhausting disk space or manipulating the archive path), which propagates through the Mono and causes the .block() in downloadSidecars() to throw, permanently halting the importer and freezing all fund-movement processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file containing a TransactionSidecarRecord with a consensus timestamp matching a token-freeze RecordItem but with an empty sidecar records case (SIDECARCORDS_NOT_SET), causing getSidecarType() to return SIDECAR_TYPE_UNKNOWN_VALUE and the record to be filtered out, leaving the freeze transaction without proper sidecar context and corrupting freeze-status data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicReference lastStreamFile at line 84 by causing a race condition between the cutoverService callback setting lastStreamFile and the concurrent downloadNextBatch() reading it, resulting in the downloader processing the same record file twice or skipping a file, permanently corrupting the block index chain and freezing fund states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar filename that, when passed to StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName()), produces a StreamFilename pointing to a different node's sidecar directory, causing the streamFileProvider to fetch an attacker-controlled sidecar file that permanently corrupts freeze-status data for targeted accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a sidecar list containing duplicate SidecarFile entries (same name, different hash), causing the Flux pipeline to process the same sidecar twice and the multimap to contain duplicate entries for the same consensus timestamp, permanently corrupting the sidecar-to-transaction mapping for freeze operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a sidecar file that causes sidecar.setBytes(null) at line 157 to be called on a SidecarFile that is still referenced by the RecordFile's items list, causing a NullPointerException during downstream parsing of token-freeze transactions and permanently halting the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file with a CONTRACT_ACTION sidecar record whose consensus timestamp matches a token-freeze RecordItem, causing the wrong sidecar type to be associated with the freeze transaction, and if the parser relies on sidecar type to determine freeze semantics, permanently misrepresenting the freeze state of the affected account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where downloadSidecars() completes successfully but associates sidecar records with the wrong RecordItems (due to timestamp collision), causing super.onVerified() to notify the streamFileNotifier with corrupted data, permanently persisting incorrect freeze-status information in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSidecar() returns a Mono<SidecarFile> and the error handling in the Flux.flatMap() at line 120 does not explicitly handle errors, causing an unhandled error signal to terminate the entire Flux and the .block() call to throw, permanently halting the importer and freezing all fund states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a sidecar whose name does not match the expected sidecar filename pattern, causing StreamFilename.from() to throw an InvalidStreamFileException that propagates through the Flux and permanently halts the importer, freezing all fund-movement processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords) call at line 121 by crafting a SidecarFile whose getRecords() returns null, causing a NullPointerException in the Flux pipeline that propagates to the .block() call and permanently halts the importer, freezing all fund-movement processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldDownload() always returns true (line 97) to ensure the downloader never self-disables, and then craft a sequence of malformed record files that each cause a recoverable error, causing the downloader to loop indefinitely on the same corrupted state and permanently freeze fund-movement processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between cutoverService.get() at line 82 and the lastStreamFile AtomicReference at line 84 by causing getLastRecordFile() to return a RecordFile with a hash that does not match the actual last processed file, causing verifyHashChain() in the parent Downloader to permanently reject all subsequent record files and freeze all fund states?
